summary: Crystal‑clear 1969 soundboard capture featuring a legendary Dark Star/Lovelight suite that blows listeners away.
review: The recording is a pristine sound‑board source that sounds crisp, bright and virtually free of the usual tape hiss of ’69 tapes.  Musically the show is dominated by an epic Dark Star that many reviewers call one of the best ever, flowing straight into a thunderous St. Stephen → The Eleven → Lovelight sequence with Steven Stills guesting.  Jerry’s lead work and Phil’s bass are repeatedly praised, making this a top‑tier early‑Dead performance.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 detailed reviews rave about both the musical peaks (Dark Star, Lovelight) and the superb sound‑board quality; only minor tape‑flip/hiss notes appear, so the combined show‑and‑recording score is near the top of the scale.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, bright mix with excellent instrument separation; a few reviewers note a tape‑flip and slight hiss that can be mitigated with treble reduction, but overall the audio is considered outstanding for 1969.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set runs like a continuous journey – a long Dark Star that segues into St. Stephen, then The Eleven and finally a relentless Lovelight.  Reviewers note the extra hour of music thanks to Daylight‑Saving time, giving the show an almost uninterrupted jam flow.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for his soaring lead guitar tone, improvisational brilliance and even his vocal work on this era.
band_member_comments.Phil: Described as “blasting through” with melodic energy; several reviewers highlight his bass lines that lock in tightly with Jerry’s solos.
